title: JobMaker Hiring Credit scheme
description: Key points about the COVID-19 support measure for eligible employers.
keywords: JobMaker, JobMaker Hiring Credit scheme, Job Maker Hiring credit, Key points for eligible employers

content_blocks.text: The JobMaker Hiring Credit scheme has ended
The JobMaker Hiring Credit scheme was an incentive for businesses to employ additional young job seekers aged 16–35 years.
Eligible employers could access the JobMaker Hiring Credit for each eligible additional employee they hired between 7 October 2020 and 6 October 2021.
The final claim period for the JobMaker Hiring credit scheme ended on 31 January 2023.
All payments under the JobMaker Hiring Credit scheme are assessable as ordinary income.
The normal deductions apply for amounts your business paid to employees if those amounts were subsidised by JobMaker Hiring Credit payments.

items: did not need to be included in your activity statements.

content_blocks.text: For businesses operating on an accruals accounting basis, JobMaker Hiring Credit payments were derived when the entity provides us with a valid claim form after each JobMaker period. The claim triggered the entity's legal entitlement to the payments.
For business entities that operate on a cash accounting basis, JobMaker Hiring Credit payments were derived when the entity received those payments.
Authorised by the Australian Government, Canberra.

content_text:
## The JobMaker Hiring Credit scheme has ended
The JobMaker Hiring Credit scheme was an incentive for businesses to employ additional young job seekers aged 16–35 years.
Eligible employers could access the JobMaker Hiring Credit for each eligible additional employee they hired between 7 October 2020 and 6 October 2021.
The final claim period for the JobMaker Hiring credit scheme ended on 31 January 2023.
## Tax consequences
All payments under the JobMaker Hiring Credit scheme are assessable as ordinary income.
The normal deductions apply for amounts your business paid to employees if those amounts were subsidised by JobMaker Hiring Credit payments.
JobMaker Hiring Credit payments were:
- not subject to GST
- did not need to be included in your activity statements.
## Operating on an accruals basis
For businesses operating on an accruals accounting basis, JobMaker Hiring Credit payments were derived when the entity provides us with a valid claim form after each JobMaker period. The claim triggered the entity's legal entitlement to the payments.
## Operating on a cash basis
For business entities that operate on a cash accounting basis, JobMaker Hiring Credit payments were derived when the entity received those payments.
Authorised by the Australian Government, Canberra.
